procedures and disposal of chemicals.

4. Students at lower level should demonstrate an ability to carry out

key experimental techniques used in the chemical and life sciences disciplines.

5. Students at upper level should be able to design experiments to test speciﬁc hypotheses, carry out these experiments using appropriate instrumentation, and analyze the results.

6. Students should demonstrate the ability to use the broader scientiﬁc literature to select appropriate information to support their work.

7. Students should effectively communicate, both verbally and in

writing, the processes of science and the results of scientiﬁc inquiry using appropriate language and models of chemistry (i.e. equations, symbolism, etc).

8. Students should understand the importance of good ethical practices
